attributes.action: Introduce an independent anonymous whistleblowing system for NHS staff
attributes.additional_details: Current “Freedom to Speak Up” processes are not fully anonymous and do not allow reliable follow-up, which may discourage staff from raising patient safety concerns. Healthcare professionals are expected to act on risks, but barriers to speaking up can delay action and increase harm. Anonymous systems used internationally show this is feasible and could support earlier reporting, improve transparency, and strengthen patient safety.
attributes.background: We call on the UK Government to introduce an independent whistleblowing system for NHS staff allowing anonymous reporting, secure communication, and case tracking. It should complement existing routes, ensure confidentiality, and improve patient safety.

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government is responsible for. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it.  
 
The Scottish Parliament has its own petition system:  
https://www.parliament.scot/get-involved/petitions
